hero.title: Resume Studio
hero.description: This page anchors the product around the fixed template, structured resume schema, and the next implementation steps for upload, tailoring, and export.

intake.title: Input requirements
intake.description: The product scope for the first version stays narrow on purpose.
items: Only your controlled template is allowed.
Users provide resume content and a target JD, not a custom layout.
The browser should edit structured sections, not free-form pages.

guardrail.title: Rewrite guardrails
guardrail.description: These rules define how the product should behave once AI tailoring is wired in.
items: Never invent facts that do not exist in the source resume.
Keep version review visible before export.
Always render the final output back into the fixed template.

sections.title: Template schema
sections.description: The first template already gives us a clear schema for extraction and editing.

notes.title: Why this matters
notes.description: These notes define the product contract before upload and export services are implemented.

studio.upload_description: Users upload their existing resume file, not a template. The system extracts text first and then turns it into structured content.
studio.helper: Currently supports PDF and DOCX. The file is attached to the fixed-template workflow.
studio.success: Resume parsed and saved to the workspace
studio.library_description: These are the resume records that already entered the fixed-template workflow.
studio.empty: No resume records yet. Upload a PDF or DOCX to create the first structured version.

editor.page_description: This editor updates the base structured resume. Saving here clears the old tailoring result so the next JD run starts from clean source data.
editor.title: Base resume editor
editor.description: Clean up the source resume first, then run JD tailoring on top of it.
editor.preview_description: This panel is driven by the same template snapshot layer as DOCX export, so the on-screen layout and the exported Word structure stay aligned.

cover_letter.title: AI Cover Letter
cover_letter.description: Generate a cover letter from the current resume and target job.

sections.skills_description: One group per line. Suggested format: Group: Summary (keyword 1 / keyword 2)
sections.strengths_description: One item per line. Suggested format: Title: Description
sections.education_description: One block per education item with a blank line between blocks. First line: School | Degree | Major | Start - End
sections.experience_description: One block per experience item with a blank line between blocks. First line: Company | Team | Role | Start - End, second line optional responsibility mix, then one bullet per line
